blocks.content: The classroom stirred.
Class Five? This wasn't just an early promotion but a jump of two levels at once.
This was a radical decision not only from the students' perspective but also from the school's.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: Teacher! I object. How can Shirone skip two classes? Shirone can't even use a single magic spell.
This was news to Siina. Etela hadn't mentioned this either. Perhaps she didn't know either, as there was no particular need to use magic in lower class lessons.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Is that true, Shirone?
The classmates turned to look at Shirone. Their eyes seemed to say: Please say you can't. Say you can't.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. I don't have any magic I can use yet.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Hmm.
Even so, there was no problem with the early promotion. Magic is taught in earnest from the upper classes, and the lower class curriculum focuses on strengthening the Spirit Zone.

Shirone has mastered the basics of the Four Direction Formation, and the size and durability of his Spirit Zone don't lag behind even compared to the upper classes. Therefore, Class Five is appropriate if we talk about the exact class.

But it was unusual that he still couldn't use magic. Students typically try magic on their own, even without being taught. After all, they entered the magic school because they desperately wanted to learn magic.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: The teacher is also curious. However, there are no evaluation items related to magic up to Class Six. Shirone's early promotion will proceed normally.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: I absolutely cannot accept this!
Mark, the implicit leader of Class Seven, stood up abruptly. He was a student with a square jaw fitting his large build.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Why are you showing favoritism to a student? Please provide convincing evidence. Teacher, you said that omniscience is as important as omnipotence! Applying special conditions only to Shirone is incomprehensible from a common-sense perspective!

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: That's right! Honestly, if I competed with Shirone on grades right now, I'm confident I would win! Shirone can't even keep up with theory lessons properly!

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: If Shirone is promoted early, I'll petition the student council to hold a hearing!

The students' resistance was much fiercer than expected. Though young, they had all risen through competition since Class Ten. I had hoped they would be stimulated by Shirone this time as well, but they were burning with hostility as if facing an enemy.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: There's something I don't know about.

From the children's expressions, which suggested they would even go to war, Siina realized the situation was serious. With this much resistance, it could actually have a negative impact on the class atmosphere.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: The decision hasn't been formally made yet. The exact announcement will be made after the teachers' meeting. That concludes today's class.
Back in the staff room, Siina rested her chin on her hand, deep in thought.
Given the nature of a specialized school, competition is inevitable. And the students knew this fact and voluntarily enrolled.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: What is it? Those strange looks from the children?
It wasn't simple jealousy. Early promotions had happened before, but never with such strong opposition.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Excuse me, Teacher Siina.
A female student named Maria came to see Siina.
The oldest student in Class Seven at 19, she had been stuck in place for 4 years without promotion.
She wasn't completely without talent, but she was timid and fearful, making her a student that the school watched with concern.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Yes? What is it, Maria?

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Actually... I have something to tell you.
After hesitating for a while, Maria spilled everything. How Shirone was being treated in the current class, and why the children were so angry just now.
As the story progressed, Siina's brow furrowed. They were bullying Shirone with methods too malicious to be seen as simple emotional conflicts.
If they encountered Shirone, they would pretend to vomit as if by agreement, and often deliberately crowded around Shirone to talk amongst themselves, excluding him.
Maria finished her story, sobbing.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Now I'm the oldest so they don't care about me, but I've actually been through it too. Back then, I couldn't focus on studying or anything. I think Shirone is amazing. How can he remain so calm in that situation?
Siina understood the whole situation. One student was being ostracized by the group. If fair competition wasn't taking place, there was no reason for the school to exist.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Thank you for telling me. From now on, the teacher will handle it.
Shirone arrived at his dormitory around midnight. Since the bullying had intensified after the early promotion talk, he thought it would be better to come in early and read books.
Knock, knock.
Someone knocked on his door. Checking the time, it was already past 1 AM.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who could it be at this hour?
Students couldn't go out, and staff had gone home. Could it be the children coming to harass him even here?
Feeling anxious, he couldn't approach the door readily, but then he heard a familiar voice from beyond the door.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Shirone, are you sleeping?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher Siina?
Shirone hurriedly opened the door. It really was Siina.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: May I come in?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, yes. Please come in.
Was today Teacher Siina's duty day? Her hair was still damp, as if she had just bathed in the dormitory.

The advanced class dormitory housed students even in their early twenties, so it wasn't a place for a female teacher to wander late at night. But Siina had chosen this time to have a private meeting away from the classmates.
Unlike other male students' rooms, everything was neatly organized. Guessing Shirone's personality, she checked the books.
